mapping.b_element: transformation or change-of-variables method for mapping the cloaked region

mapping.a_element: designing the potential and effective mass of matter waves in the cloaking region
mapping.b_element: anisotropic mass density or mechanical stiffness in the acoustic cloak

mapping.a_element: wave and particle (classical) approaches confirming cloaking conditions
mapping.b_element: phase and wave speeds of pseudo-acoustic waves in the cloak being unique

mapping.a_element: optical lattices for cold atoms as a realization
mapping.b_element: pentamode materials as a realization of anisotropic stiffness

brief_justification: Both papers employ the transformation or change-of-variables method to achieve cloaking by designing spatially varying material properties (potential/effective mass in quantum mechanics, anisotropic density/stiffness in acoustics). The core machinery—coordinate transformation to suppress scattering—is identical, with domain-specific parameters playing analogous roles.
